name: Complex component with many axes — tests variant architecture decisions

input.userMessage: Plan a data table row component. It needs to support: selectable rows (checkbox), expandable rows (arrow to show details), different row heights (compact/default/relaxed), row states (default/selected/hover/disabled), and cells can contain text, numbers, badges, avatars, or action buttons

expectedBehaviors: Identifies that naive variant matrix would be enormous (2×2×3×4 = 48 variants before cell types)
Recommends boolean props for selectable and expandable (not variant axes — they're on/off toggles)
Keeps variant axes to: Height (3 values) × State (4 values) = 12 variants
Plans cell content as instance swap slots, not variants
Studies existing table/table-cell/table-header components for convention matching
Plans relationship with existing table components (this is contained by Table)
antiPatterns: Creates a 48+ variant matrix by making everything a variant axis
Uses variants for selectable/expandable instead of boolean props
Creates separate variant for each cell content type
Ignores existing table components in the library

evaluationCriteria.variantArchitecture: Does it use the RIGHT mechanism (variant vs boolean vs swap) for each property?
evaluationCriteria.matrixManagement: Does it keep variant count reasonable (<20)?
evaluationCriteria.conventionMatching: Does it study existing table components for patterns?
evaluationCriteria.compositionAwareness: Does it plan how this fits with existing Table and Table cell?
